result:
Done. `AddWithPreemption` is implemented in `Scheduler.cs:150` and the project builds cleanly with no warnings.

The implementation follows the spec's four steps: it rejects invalid intervals and any case where an overlapping active booking has priority ≥ the new one (both return −1 with no state changes); otherwise it deactivates all overlapping bookings, records the new booking at index `count`, then re-places each displaced booking in increasing index order using the existing `NextFreeStart` with `earliest = e` — so each re-placement sees the new booking and previously re-placed ones — cancelling any booking that can't fit before `horizon`. When nothing overlaps, it degenerates to exactly `AddBooking` behavior, with no horizon check on the new booking itself. No existing function was touched, and it stays within the file's conventions (while loops, pure integer computation, no I/O).

I verified behavior with a throwaway console project (outside the source tree, since test files aren't allowed) against the compiled DLL: cascading displacement (`[15,25)` p3 pushing two bookings to `[25,35)` and `[35,45)`), equal-priority rejection, invalid-interval rejection, no-conflict adds ignoring the horizon, horizon-driven cancellation of displaced bookings, and correctness of `ActiveCount`/`PriorityAt`/`BusyTime` plus the no-overlap invariant on the resulting state. All cases matched the expected values, and the scratch project has been deleted. Used 2 of the 10 build/test cycles.
